pulse: The energy sector currently trades at a median EV/EBITDA of 11.1x and a median P/E of 17.9x, aligning closely with broader market averages. Notably, Exxon Mobil (XOM) is seeing premarket gains with an EV/EBITDA of 11.5x and a trailing P/E of 22.8x, driven by recent spikes in oil prices to six-month highs due to escalating Middle East tensions, which have raised concerns over the stability of global energy supplies. Given these dynamics, investors may want to consider shifting allocations towards energy stocks, as geopolitical risks increasingly position energy assets as critical national security resources and could boost profitability in the sector.
